summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Board Game Score (React+FastAPI+Mongo+JWT+WebSockets). Backend: 17/18 pytest tests pass. Only failure is brute-force lockout (real bug — uses request.client.host which is K8s ingress IP that varies per request, so attempts never accumulate to 5). Frontend: full e2e admin flow works — login → start game → submit scores → next turn → end game → history; library catalog add works; stats 4 tabs render; export buttons present; WS waiting screen on /. No console errors during flows.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout never triggers behind K8s ingress. backend uses request.client.host which varies (saw 10.227.7.2 and 10.227.7.6 alternating in db.login_attempts for the same external client+email). Need to honor X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP. Currently the 5-fail lockout protection is effectively disabled in production-like environments.

On the 5th failed attempt, code sets locked_until AND resets attempts=0 but still returns 401 rather than 429 on that same response. Minor UX nit — the 5th failure is silently the trigger; the user only sees 429 on the 6th. Consider returning 429 directly on the locking attempt.

issues: Crown icon appears next to the player at current_turn_idx rather than the current leader by score. With Highest-wins selected, the crown sits on Bob (15) instead of Carol (20). If intended as a turn indicator it's fine; if it's meant to mark the leader it's misleading.

action_items: Fix brute-force IP detection: derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For (first hop) / X-Real-IP header in /api/auth/login so the 5-fail lockout actually works behind the K8s ingress.
(Optional polish) Return 429 on the attempt that triggers the lock, not only on subsequent attempts.
(Optional UX) Clarify crown icon semantics on Admin live game card (turn indicator vs leader).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~600 lines and starting to mix concerns (auth, catalog, game, history, players, WS). Consider splitting into routers (auth.py, game.py, catalog.py, ws.py) before it grows further.

Cookie security flags use secure=False with samesite=lax — fine for the preview environment, but for prod should be secure=True + samesite=none (since frontend and API are on the same eTLD here it's currently OK).
Login lockout uses request.client.host; behind any reverse proxy this gives the proxy IP. Use X-Forwarded-For with a trusted-proxy check.

/api/auth/refresh only reads the cookie; the frontend uses Bearer-only flow and stores no refresh token, so refresh effectively requires the httpOnly cookie to still be present. Works because login does set the cookie too, but worth documenting.

broadcast_current_game() sends None when there is no current game (good). However ws_endpoint loops on receive_text() — clients that don't send heartbeats are fine, but any binary frame would crash silently. Acceptable for now.
test_credentials: admin@boardgame.app / admin123 (verified working, role=admin, access_token returned, password_hash NOT in response)
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST_ prefixed catalog/history/players during tests. Cleaned up after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests run with `pytest backend/tests/test_board_game_api.py -n 0` (serial). The default pytest.ini uses -n 2 --dist loadscope which races on the single global current_game doc — tests must use -n 0 OR be redesigned to isolate state. WebSocket tests use wss:// derived from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend e2e via the admin UI works end-to-end. Public scoreboard (/) shows waiting screen with orbiting dice when no current_game.
rca of the issue: Brute-force lockout failure RCA: db.login_attempts stores identifier=f'{request.client.host}:{email}'. In a K8s ingress / load-balancer environment, FastAPI's request.client.host receives the connection-level peer IP, which is the ingress/nginx pod IP and varies across connections (observed 10.227.7.2 and 10.227.7.6 alternating for the same external client). Each new IP gets a fresh attempts counter, so 5 fails never accumulate against one record and locked_until is never set. Mitigation: read X-Forwarded-For (split by comma, take leftmost) or X-Real-IP, with a trusted-proxy allowlist; fallback to request.client.host only when headers are absent. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login with wrong password for the same email 10x from the same external client → no 429 returned, db shows multiple identifier rows each with attempts < 5.
